title: The Call of Abraham
objective: Understand God's promise to Abraham and the beginning of the covenant people.

sections.content: God called Abram to leave his homeland and follow Him. Through Abram, God promised to bless all nations.

sections.text: I will make of you a great nation, and I will bless you.

sections.heading: God's Covenant Promise
sections.content: God promised Abraham land, descendants, and blessing. This promise ultimately points to Christ.
God keeps His promises across generations.

questions: Why did God call Abraham?
What promises did God make?
How does this point toward Jesus?

sections.content: Lord, strengthen our faith like Abraham's. Amen.

title: Abraham's Faith
objective: Learn how Abraham trusted God even when the promise seemed impossible.

sections.content: Abraham believed God's promise even when it seemed impossible.

sections.text: And he believed the Lord, and he counted it to him as righteousness.

sections.heading: Faith and Promise
sections.content: Faith means trusting God's promise even when circumstances seem uncertain.
Faith trusts in God's promise of grace.

questions: What does Abraham's faith teach us?
Why is faith important?
How do we trust God today?

sections.content: Lord, help us trust Your promises. Amen.

title: Isaac the Promised Son
objective: Understand how God fulfilled His promise through Isaac.

sections.content: God fulfilled His promise by giving Abraham and Sarah a son named Isaac.

sections.text: The Lord visited Sarah as he had said.

sections.heading: God Keeps His Word
sections.content: Isaac's birth shows that God always keeps His promises.
God's promises never fail.

questions: Why was Isaac's birth important?
What promise did it fulfill?
What does it teach about God's faithfulness?

sections.content: Faithful God, thank You for keeping Your promises. Amen.

title: Abraham and Isaac
objective: See how Abraham's obedience points toward God's sacrifice of His Son.

sections.content: God tested Abraham by asking him to sacrifice Isaac.

sections.text: Now I know that you fear God.

sections.heading: A Picture of Christ
sections.content: God ultimately provided a substitute sacrifice, pointing forward to Jesus.
Christ is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world.

questions: Why did God test Abraham?
What does the substitute sacrifice represent?
How does this story foreshadow Christ?

sections.content: Lord, thank You for providing the true sacrifice for our sins. Amen.

title: Jacob and Esau
objective: Understand God's covenant continuing through Jacob.

sections.content: Jacob and Esau were twin brothers, but God chose Jacob to continue the covenant promise.

sections.text: The older shall serve the younger.

sections.heading: God's Sovereign Choice
sections.content: God works through imperfect people to fulfill His promises.
God's grace works even through human weakness.

questions: Why was Jacob chosen?
What does this teach about God's plan?
How does God use imperfect people?

sections.content: Lord, guide us according to Your will. Amen.

title: Jacob's Dream
objective: Learn about God's promise reaffirmed to Jacob.

sections.content: Jacob dreamed of a ladder reaching to heaven, reminding him of God's presence.

sections.text: Behold, I am with you and will keep you wherever you go.

sections.heading: God Is With His People
sections.content: God reassured Jacob that His covenant promise would continue.
God promises to be with His people.

questions: What did Jacob see in the dream?
What promise did God make?
How does God guide us today?

sections.content: God, remind us that You are always with us. Amen.

title: Joseph Sold Into Egypt
objective: Understand how jealousy led Joseph's brothers to betray him.

sections.content: Joseph's brothers became jealous and sold him into slavery.

sections.text: They drew Joseph up and lifted him out of the pit and sold him.

sections.heading: Sin and Betrayal
sections.content: Even painful events can be used by God to accomplish His purposes.
God can bring good even out of evil.

questions: Why were Joseph's brothers jealous?
What happened to Joseph?
How can God work through hardship?

sections.content: Lord, help us trust You even during hardship. Amen.

title: Joseph Forgives His Brothers
objective: See how God's plan worked through Joseph to preserve His people.

sections.content: Joseph rose to power in Egypt and later forgave his brothers.

sections.text: You meant evil against me, but God meant it for good.

sections.heading: God's Providence
sections.content: God used Joseph's suffering to save many lives.
God's providence guides history.

questions: How did Joseph forgive his brothers?
What does this teach about God's plan?
Why is forgiveness important?

sections.content: Lord, help us forgive others as You forgive us. Amen.
